change_summary: Since c9 (2026-04-22T11:33Z), three integrations and one parallel patch have landed against WIN-067 detail_evidence: (a) EXP-221 Patch A — added the explicit Domain 4 self-falsification sentence quoting g(20015) ≈ 9.7854 m/s² and noting the 4.8× exceedance of the dome's own 0.1% threshold; (b) EXP-221 Patch B — added the Mascon↔SAA r=0.92 rebuttal paragraph attributing the co-location to East Antarctic crustal roots + sub-lithospheric mantle plume (citing Pappa et al. 2019); (c) EXP-223 — added a closing refraction-cascade sentence anchoring H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km at the rim; (d) parallel patch a3bf2c4 (just landed during this review) — Decider patches ISS-1358/1375 by clarifying the Shepherd 2012 citation as 'ice-sheet mass loss; GIA adjustment modeled from LGM deglaciation loading history', resolving the c9 H4 misattribution finding. detail_evidence_length grew 7155→9098 (+27%, well past the 20% re-review threshold). c9 H1 (duplicate sentence) and c9 H2 (170× isolation) are both fixed; c9 H3 (arithmetic) is partially fixed in detail_evidence but a residual phrase persists in tldr_evidence.

prior_findings_status.label: H1 — duplicate Pappa sentence
prior_findings_status.evidence: Patch B replaced the redundant duplicate with a fully-developed Mascon↔SAA paragraph; only one Pappa citation per topic now appears in the relevant region.

prior_findings_status.label: H2 — closing 170× isolation
prior_findings_status.evidence: Closing now reads '170–1,430× depending on the comparison' with explicit reconciliation of which baseline produces which factor; no longer reads as a softer single number.

prior_findings_status.label: H3 — arithmetic precision in tldr_evidence
prior_findings_status.evidence: tldr_evidence still contains the phrase 'by this 0.006 m/s² measured drop gives 1,430×' — the 0.006 figure is rounded from 0.0062, and the arithmetic 8.87/0.006 = 1,478 ≠ 1,430. detail_evidence is correct (uses 0.0062). Carry forward as new H2 (MINOR).

prior_findings_status.label: H4 — Shepherd citation misattribution
prior_findings_status.evidence: Decider commit a3bf2c4 (2026-04-24, ISS-1358/1375) inserted the parenthetical 'Shepherd et al., 2012, Science — ice-sheet mass loss; GIA adjustment modeled from LGM deglaciation loading history' which decouples the Shepherd citation (mass-balance) from the GIA modeling claim. Drop the planned re-flagging.

prior_findings_status.label: H5 — advocate-mode rebuttal opportunity (Mascon-SAA)
prior_findings_status.evidence: EXP-221 Patch B is exactly the rebuttal paragraph c9 advocate_mode requested. The Mascon↔SAA r=0.92 attack surface is now engaged on its own terms.

holes_found.location: detail_evidence, Patch B closing paragraph (last ~600 chars)
holes_found.description: Patch B cites Pappa et al. 2019 — an Antarctic crustal-gravity paper — as the source for the *shared cause* of both the Mascon negative gravity anomaly AND the South Atlantic Anomaly (SAA) field minimum. Pappa et al. is a clean and correct citation for the Antarctic crustal/mantle contribution to the gravity anomaly, but mainstream geomagnetism attributes the SAA itself to core-dynamo dynamics — specifically reverse-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (Tarduno et al., Finlay et al., IGRF model decompositions) — not to lithospheric or upper-mantle structure. The text reads '...through the associated lithospheric/mantle structure, contribute to the geometry of the SAA field minimum', which overstates Pappa's scope into the magnetic-field domain it does not address. A defender (or peer reviewer) can correctly point out that we are leveraging one citation across two physically distinct fields whose causes are not co-explained by Pappa.
holes_found.fix: Either (a) add a second citation for the SAA attribution — e.g., Finlay et al. 2020 IGRF or Tarduno et al. on reverse-flux patches — and reword to 'Pappa et al. for the gravity anomaly; reverse-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (Finlay et al., 2020) for the SAA' so the two fields get their own mainstream sources; or (b) soften the linkage to 'plausible co-localization through deep crustal/lithospheric heterogeneity' without claiming Pappa explains the SAA.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence reads '...by this 0.006 m/s² measured drop gives 1,430×'. The actual measured drop is 0.0062 m/s² and 8.87/0.0062 = 1,430.6 (correct). The TLDR rounds 0.0062 → 0.006, which would give 8.87/0.006 = 1,478×, not 1,430×. The conclusion is right, but the displayed division is internally inconsistent with the displayed numerator. detail_evidence does not have this issue.
holes_found.fix: Replace '0.006 m/s²' with '0.0062 m/s²' in tldr_evidence (single-character edit). Same fix proposed in c9; appears to have been applied to detail_evidence only.

holes_found.location: detail_evidence, Patch A sentence on Domain 4 falsification
holes_found.description: Patch A states that g(20015) ≈ 9.7854 m/s² 'violates Domain 4's own published falsification criterion ("Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard") by 4.8×' but does not show the 4.8× derivation. The reader has to compute (9.8322 − 9.7854)/9.8322 = 0.476%, then 0.476/0.1 = 4.76× ≈ 4.8×. A skim-reader (or AI summarizer) sees an unsupported '4.8×' claim. Given that the surrounding paragraph is otherwise meticulous about showing arithmetic, the asymmetry is conspicuous. A dome defender's first move on Patch A will be 'show your work' — and we leave it as an exercise.
holes_found.fix: Insert one parenthetical: 'violates Domain 4's own published falsification criterion (Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard) by 4.8× — (9.8322 − 9.7854)/9.8322 = 0.476%, i.e. 4.76× the 0.1% threshold.' Adds ~70 chars and pre-empts the show-your-work attack.

holes_found.description: The new closing sentence reads: 'The refraction cascade (Section 1.8) adds a further constraint: at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 15,500–20,015 km), the firmament ceiling H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km.' This is a complete subject-verb-object statement but does not actually deliver the constraint it announces. It promises 'a further constraint' (constraining what? the gravity prediction? the dome model? the comparison?) and then states a height range with no follow-through. A reader asking 'so what?' gets no answer. The sentence reads as a placeholder where a synthesis sentence was intended to follow.
holes_found.fix: Either complete the thought — e.g., 'adds a further constraint: at the Antarctic rim, H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km, so the linear-coupling prediction collapses gravity to a fraction inconsistent with WGS84 at any latitude in this range — closing the last loophole that variable H could hide the discrepancy' — or remove the orphan and let the prior paragraph carry the close. Current state is the worst of both: it claims more than the body delivers.

holes_found.description: Text states '~5,600×' for the 80°S anchor scenario (8.87/0.0016). Actual ratio is 8.87/0.0016 = 5,544. Rounding 5,544 → 5,600 is a 1% upward adjustment in our favor. Not large, but inconsistent with the surrounding precision (1,430×, 170×, 590× are all stated to three significant figures). Pedantic but findable.
holes_found.fix: Change '~5,600×' → '~5,500×' (round-down to the same precision) or '~5,540×' (three sig fig). Either matches the rest of the precision band.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender, accepting our internal-consistency principle, would attack Patch B head-on: 'Your closing paragraph cites Pappa et al. 2019 — an Antarctic gravity paper — as the cause of the SAA. The SAA is a geomagnetic field anomaly; Pappa addresses crustal density structure. You are doing exactly what you accuse the dome of: stretching one citation across two physically distinct domains. The mainstream attribution for the SAA is reverse-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary, not lithospheric structure. Until you supply a second citation for the SAA itself, your r=0.92 rebuttal is no more rigorous than the dome\'s "Sub-terrestrial structure" hand-wave — you are just dressing it in mainstream clothes.' This is rhetorically effective because (a) it uses our own methodology against us, (b) it identifies a real overreach (Pappa does not address the SAA), and (c) it equates our citation discipline to the dome's, undermining the contrast we are trying to draw.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Resolve via H1 fix: split the citation into (i) Pappa et al. 2019 for the gravity-anomaly side and (ii) a core-dynamo/SAA-specific reference (Finlay et al. 2020 IGRF, or Tarduno et al. on reverse-flux patches) for the SAA attribution. Reword the linking phrase from 'through the associated lithospheric/mantle structure, contribute to the geometry of the SAA field minimum' to something like 'while the SAA itself is independently explained by reverse-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (Finlay et al., 2020) — the two anomalies share a region of geological complexity but have distinct physical causes, and the dome supplies an equation for neither.' This converts the defender's strongest attack into our reinforcing point: the co-location is geographic coincidence over deep heterogeneity, not a single mechanism, and the dome still has zero equations.

cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-068 (gravity-prediction interior anomaly) uses the same WGS84 baselines and the same H(r) collapse argument; WIN-067 and WIN-068 are mutually reinforcing. WIN-010 (Schumann/spherical-Earth derivation) similarly attacks 'derived-from-globe formulas relabeled' — the methodology in WIN-067 Patch A (Domain 4 derives from WGS84) is the same charge as WIN-010 (Schumann derives from globe Earth–ionosphere cavity). WIN-025 (mantle/core composition) shares the mainstream-geophysics citation pattern but does not invoke Pappa or Shepherd, so no cross-citation conflicts. No cross-WIN contradictions identified.

claims_checked: g(20015) = 9.7854 m/s² at the Antarctic rim radius → computed 9.78535 ✓
8.87/0.0098 = 905× (alternative free-air-anomaly anchor) — not used, but consistent
8.87/0.0016 = 5,544 → claimed '~5,600×' — UPWARD ROUNDING (~1% in our favor); flagged as H5
590× under H^(1/5) scaling — verified consistent with (10)^(1/5) ≈ 1.585, gravity drop ratio ≈ (1 − 1/1.585) ≈ 36.9% predicted vs 0.063% measured = 587× ≈ 590× ✓
H(20015) ≈ 837 km consistent with claimed range '1,400–840 km' ✓
quantitative_verification.computations: All ratios re-derived in python from first principles (WGS84 Somigliana formula, Domain 4 formula at φ=90° and r=20015 km, baseline differentials at 70°S/80°S). Formula evaluations match published WIN-067 detail_evidence values to within rounding everywhere except H5 (5,544 → '~5,600×').
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Single rounding inconsistency at the '~5,600×' figure (true value 5,544); flagged as H5 MINOR. All other quantitative claims verified to displayed precision. The 4.8× claim in Patch A is correct but undocumented in-text (flagged as H3 — show-your-work).

summary_for_decider: WIN-067 is in good shape after EXP-221 (Patches A+B), EXP-223, and parallel patch a3bf2c4. Three of four c9 findings are fully fixed; one (Shepherd) was superseded by today's parallel decider patch. Five new findings, all small: one MODERATE that needs attention (H1 — Pappa overcite into SAA domain — defender's strongest counter-attack) and one MODERATE that's a minor edit (H4 — hanging refraction sentence). Two MODERATE/MINOR are show-your-work fixes (H2, H3) and one is a rounding tweak (H5). Self-Contradicted verdict stands; if anything Patch A's explicit Domain-4 falsification quote strengthens it. Recommend routing H1 + H4 as a single small expansion (defender-preemption + sentence completion) and H2/H3/H5 as a single text-only patch.
review_lens_notes: Lens A produced the most actionable finding (H1) — without explicit advocate-mode role-play we would have missed that Patch B uses Pappa et al. across two physically distinct fields. Lens B (cross-WIN) found WIN-067/068/010 mutually reinforcing with no contradictions. Lens C (quantitative) confirmed all displayed math except the H5 rounding.
